description_long: This is a vintage military biplane with a distinctive twin-boom tail and a central fuselage, painted in a camouflage pattern of tan, green, and white with Royal Air Force roundels on the wings and fuselage. The aircraft features two large propeller engines mounted on the leading edge of the lower wing, and a fixed undercarriage with visible wheels. The wings are straight and positioned in a biplane configuration, with the upper wing slightly forward of the lower. The tail section is connected by two booms extending from the wings, supporting a horizontal stabilizer and a single vertical fin. The cockpit is located at the front of the fuselage and appears to be enclosed, with a clear canopy. The overall design suggests a World War II-era reconnaissance or light attack aircraft.
description: A vintage military biplane with twin booms, camouflage paint, and dual propeller engines, featuring RAF roundels and a front-mounted enclosed cockpit.
description_view_0: The left side view highlights the twin-boom tail, biplane wings, and RAF roundels on the wings and fuselage.
description_view_1: The front view shows the dual propeller engines, fixed landing gear, and the central cockpit canopy.
description_view_2: The right side view displays the twin-boom structure, camouflage pattern, and the placement of the cockpit and engines.
description_view_3: The rear view emphasizes the twin-boom tail, horizontal stabilizer, and the alignment of the biplane wings.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with straight, mid-mounted wings and no visible winglets; slight dihedral on upper wing.
attribute_engine_type: Twin piston engines mounted on the lower wing leading edges, each with a two-bladed propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Twin-boom tail with a single vertical stabilizer and a horizontal stabilizer connecting the booms; conventional tailplane layout.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a small tail wheel.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with a clear canopy, likely single-seat.
